([Estonian](/wiki/Estonian_language "Estonian language"): *Eesti Meremuuseum*) is located in the [Fat Margaret](/wiki/Fat_Margaret "Fat Margaret") tower in the [old town](/wiki/Tallinn%27s_Old_Town "Tallinn's Old Town") of [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"). The museum presents the history of ships and navigation in [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") and related to Estonia. Other parts of the Maritime Museum are the mine museum and the [Seaplane Harbour](/wiki/Seaplane_Harbour "Seaplane Harbour") museum where [ships](/wiki/Museum_ship "Museum ship") are presented.[[2]](#cite_note-Eesti_Meremuuseum-2) The museum claims to be one of the largest museums in Estonia and the most popular.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Museum in Tallinn, Estonia

|  |  |

Estonian Maritime Museum

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d1/Paks_Margareeta_%28Fat_Margaret%29_%28377463242%29.jpg/250px-Paks_Margareeta_%28Fat_Margaret%29_%28377463242%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Paks_Margareeta_(Fat_Margaret)_(377463242).jpg)

Fat Margaret tower

The **Estonian Maritime Museum** ([Estonian](/wiki/Estonian_language "Estonian language"): *Eesti Meremuuseum*) is located in the [Fat Margaret](/wiki/Fat_Margaret "Fat Margaret") tower in the [old town](/wiki/Tallinn%27s_Old_Town "Tallinn's Old Town") of [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"). The museum presents the history of ships and navigation in [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") and related to Estonia. Other parts of the Maritime Museum are the mine museum and the [Seaplane Harbour](/wiki/Seaplane_Harbour "Seaplane Harbour") museum where [ships](/wiki/Museum_ship "Museum ship") are presented.[[2]](#cite_note-Eesti_Meremuuseum-2) The museum claims to be one of the largest museums in Estonia and the most popular.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Estonian_Maritime_Museum&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Museum was established in February 1935 by former captains and sailors.[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4) In November 1940, after the [Soviet occupation of Estonia](/wiki/German_occupation_of_Estonia_during_World_War_II "German occupation of Estonia during World War II") began, the museum was closed and its collection moved to the [Kiek in de Kök tower](/wiki/Kiek_in_de_K%C3%B6k,_Tallinn "Kiek in de Kök, Tallinn"). The original museum building was destroyed in the war.

After [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), the museum's collection was distributed to [Tallinn City Museum](/wiki/Tallinn_City_Museum "Tallinn City Museum") and other local museums. In 1961, the museum was reestablished. In 1977, as part of a restoration plan of the [Old Town](/wiki/Tallinn_Old_Town "Tallinn Old Town") in preparation for the upcoming [1980 Olympic Games in Moscow](/wiki/1980_Summer_Olympics "1980 Summer Olympics"), the museum was again closed and reopened in April 1981.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Since 1981, its main exhibition has been housed by the Fat Margaret tower. A second museum was opened in May 2012 in the [Seaplane Harbor](/wiki/Seaplane_Harbour "Seaplane Harbour").[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4)

## Fat Margaret

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Estonian_Maritime_Museum&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Fat Margaret")]

Main article: [Fat Margaret](/wiki/Fat_Margaret "Fat Margaret")

[Fat Margaret](/wiki/Fat_Margaret "Fat Margaret") (Estonian: *Paks Margareeta*, also known in German as *Dicke Margarethe*) was built in the early 16th century (from 1511 to 1530) during the reconstruction of the medieval city gate system. The etymology of the tower's name derives from the fact that it was the largest part of the city's fortifications with walls measuring 25 meters in diameter, 20 meters in height and up to 5 meters thick. Apart from being a fortification against would-be invaders to the port of the town, it was also built to impress outside visitors arriving by sea.[[6]](#cite_note-Tallinn_Tourism-6)

## Exhibitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Estonian_Maritime_Museum&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Exhibitions")]

Present exhibits include[[2]](#cite_note-Eesti_Meremuuseum-2)[*[dead link](/wiki/Wikipedia:Link_rot "Wikipedia:Link rot")*]

* [EML *Lembit*](/wiki/EML_Lembit "EML Lembit") - A World War II *Kalev*-class submarine
* EML *Grif* - A [*Zhuk*-class patrol boat](/wiki/Zhuk-class_patrol_boat "Zhuk-class patrol boat")
* [EML *Kalev* (M414)](/wiki/EML_Kalev_(M414) "EML Kalev (M414)") - A [*Frauenlob*-class minesweeper](/w/index.php?title=Frauenlob-class_minesweeper&action=edit&redlink=1 "Frauenlob-class minesweeper (page does not exist)")
* PVL 105 *Torm* - A [*Storm*-class patrol boat](/wiki/Storm-class_patrol_boat "Storm-class patrol boat") used by the coastguard.
* PVL 106 *Maru*
* PVL 109 *Valvas* - An [Iris-class](/wiki/USCG_seagoing_buoy_tender "USCG seagoing buoy tender") patrol boat used by the coastguard, originally known as the [USCGC Bittersweet](/wiki/USCGC_Bittersweet_(WLB-389) "USCGC Bittersweet (WLB-389)")
* EML Suurop (P421) - A [R-class patrol boat](/wiki/R-class_patrol_boat "R-class patrol boat")
* [*Suur Tõll*](/wiki/Suur_T%C3%B5ll_(icebreaker) "Suur Tõll (icebreaker)") - a steamer-icebreaker built in 1914
* Research vessel *Mare*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8b/EML_Kalev_%28M414%29%2C_ship.JPG/250px-EML_Kalev_%28M414%29%2C_ship.JPG)](/wiki/File:EML_Kalev_(M414),_ship.JPG)

[EML *Kalev* (M414)](/wiki/EML_Kalev_(M414) "EML Kalev (M414)")
